title: QuickSend
description: Professional DSP takedown service for beatmakers and music creators. We remove stolen beats, covers and videos from streaming platforms quickly and reliably.
whyDesc: We protect beatmakers, producers, and artists from copyright theft on every major streaming platform.
productsSubtitle: Powerful tools for effective Email Sending and other
pricingSub: First takedown is free. Then $25 per takedown.
feedback: Send us your questions, improvement suggestions or bug reports. We reply within 24 hours.

pricing.subtitle: Transparent pay-per-takedown pricing. No subscriptions, no hidden fees.
pricing.freeBannerTitle: Your First Takedown is on Us
pricing.freeBannerDesc: Sign in with Google, submit your first copyright complaint, and we will handle it at no cost.
pricing.bundleDescSingle: Perfect for reporting a one-off leak.
pricing.bundleDescPack5: Best for creators who file takedowns regularly.
pricing.bundleDescPack10: Best value for labels and management teams.
pricing.feature1: Takedown filed on Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube, SoundCloud, Tidal, Deezer, Amazon Music or other platforms.
pricing.feature2: Covers beats, cover art, music videos and other copyright-protected content.
pricing.feature3: Dedicated manager reviews every report personally before submission.
pricing.feature4: Status updates sent to your profile and email throughout the process.
pricing.feature5: Average removal confirmed within 3 to 7 business days.
pricing.creditsNote: Credits are added to your balance instantly and never expire. Use them whenever you need a takedown filed.
pricing.securePayment: Payments are encrypted and processed by our providers — we never store your card details.
pricing.step1Desc: Choose a pack. Credits land in your balance the moment payment is confirmed.
pricing.step2Desc: Fill out a short form — one credit is reserved and our manager reviews it personally.
pricing.step3Desc: We file the takedown on the platform. Most tracks come down within 3–7 business days.
pricing.homeSubtitle: Buy takedown credits once, use them whenever you need. No recurring charges, no hidden fees.
pricing.noSubscriptionNote: No subscriptions. You only pay for the takedowns you actually need.

advantages.speedDesc: We submit takedown requests within 24 hours of receiving your report.
advantages.securityDesc: All reports follow DMCA and international copyright law requirements.
advantages.fastResultDesc: Average track removal takes 3 to 7 business days after submission.
advantages.lowPriceDesc: First takedown is free. Pay only $25 per takedown. Volume discounts available.
advantages.legalDesc: We guide you through providing the right proof of ownership for maximum success.
advantages.supportDesc: Our team reviews every report and keeps you updated at every step.
advantages.flexibleSetupDesc: Configure email sending to your needs

products.massEmailsDesc: Our mass email service can break Gmail sending limits using our distribution technology.
products.mailMergeDesc: Quicksend will read data live from any Google Sheet and even monitor for new rows and then send emails automatically.
products.emailVerificationDesc: Verify your list, test links, & fix spam triggers and invalid emails before you send
products.scheduling: Schedule Mass emails and Mail Merges
products.schedulingDesc: Schedule a mail merge for any time in the future, or set it to repeat daily or hourly.
products.personalizationDesc: Connect to a Google Sheet and personalize with any column, or use basic first and last name personalization without a spreadsheet. Use fallback values. Do not know someones first name? We will auto-detect it.
products.followUpsDesc: Sending cold email? Set automatic follow-up emails to send in a sequence until your recipient replies. The best way to boost response rates.

login.joinCommunity: Sign in to submit takedown requests, track their status, and protect your music.

terms.title: QuickSend Terms of Service
terms.intro:
Please note that your use of and access to our services (defined below) is governed by the following terms; if you do not agree to all of the terms below, you may not use or access the services in any manner.

These Terms of Service ("Terms") are a binding agreement between you and QuickSend, Inc. ("QuickSend", "we", "us"). QuickSend provides a service for removing music tracks, cover art, and videos from DSP platforms (Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and other music services) based on copyright infringement complaints (the "Services"). If you have any questions, comments, or concerns about these Terms or the Services, contact us at quicksendcontact@gmail.com.

By using the Services in any way, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms, including the Privacy Policy.
terms.philosophyText: We aim to make copyright protection for musicians and rights holders as fast, reliable, and transparent as possible. These Terms are meant to ensure a good and fair experience for both you and us.
terms.serviceDescriptionText: QuickSend accepts requests from users to remove specific tracks, cover art, or videos from DSP platforms, and manually prepares and submits the corresponding copyright infringement complaint on behalf of the rights holder. The status of each request is shown in your account dashboard. We are not a DSP platform and do not control the timeline or final decision on any complaint — the decision to remove content rests with the relevant platform.
terms.ownershipRequirementText: By submitting a request through QuickSend, you confirm that you are the rights holder of the relevant work (author, performer, label, publisher, etc.) or are authorized to act on their behalf, and that the information and documents you provide are accurate. You are required to provide materials supporting your rights (contracts, split sheets, copyright registrations, correspondence with a distributor, or other documents) needed to prepare the complaint. Knowingly submitting a false claim of copyright infringement may result in legal liability to QuickSend and to third parties, and may be grounds for refusing service or suspending your account.
terms.processText: After payment and submission of your request, our team manually reviews the links and documents you provided, may request clarification if needed, and then prepares and submits a copyright infringement complaint to the relevant DSP platform. The status of your request (in review, submitted, removed, rejected by platform, etc.) is shown in your account dashboard. We notify you when the status changes.
terms.noGuaranteeText: We make reasonable efforts to submit complaints promptly and correctly, but we cannot guarantee that a DSP platform will grant the complaint, and we cannot guarantee any specific removal timeline, since the decision and review timing are entirely determined by the relevant platform's own policies and processes and are outside our control. If a platform rejects a complaint due to insufficient proof of rights, we may offer to resubmit it once additional supporting material is provided.
terms.paymentsText: QuickSend's services are pay-per-use; there is no subscription. A single takedown (removal of a track across all associated DSP platforms) costs $25 USD. Bundles are also available: 5 takedowns for $110 USD, and 10 takedowns for $190 USD. Payments are accepted via Stripe, PayPal, and YooKassa. Purchased takedowns ("credits") do not expire unless stated otherwise.
terms.refundsText: Once your request has been accepted and the complaint has been submitted to a DSP platform, the corresponding credit is considered used and is non-refundable, since the work has already been performed, regardless of the platform's final decision. Unused credits (for which a request has not yet been submitted) can be refunded upon request — email us at quicksendcontact@gmail.com.
terms.chargebacksText:
Occasionally a user initiates a chargeback through their bank or payment provider instead of contacting us directly. As a small business, we take chargebacks and the costs they create seriously, and as a result:

1. We investigate the incident to determine its cause.
2. If we believe the chargeback is unwarranted, we submit evidence of completed work (the submitted complaint, correspondence with the platform) to the bank or payment provider.
3. We may suspend the account associated with the chargeback until the dispute is resolved.

We recommend always contacting our support first — most issues are resolved faster this way than through a bank dispute.
terms.supportText: You can reach our support team at quicksendcontact@gmail.com or through your account dashboard. We aim to respond to inquiries about request status, payments, and service operation within a reasonable time.
terms.confidentialityText:
Documents proving your rights to a work that you submit with a request are used by QuickSend solely to prepare and submit a copyright infringement complaint and to help resolve potential disputes (for example, if the uploader files a counter-notice). We do not publish or share these documents with third parties, except as necessary for the removal process itself (for example, submitting them to a DSP platform as an attachment to the complaint) or as required by law.

You may voluntarily provide feedback about the Services to QuickSend. QuickSend may use such feedback to improve the Services.
terms.privacyText: QuickSend takes the privacy of user data seriously, including documents proving rights to a work. Full details on what data we collect and how we use it are set out in our Privacy Policy.
terms.warrantyText: THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED BY QUICKSEND ON AN "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR THAT ANY COMPLAINT WILL BE GRANTED BY A GIVEN PLATFORM. QUICKSEND DOES NOT GUARANTEE THAT ANY CONTENT WILL BE REMOVED, AS THE FINAL DECISION RESTS WITH THE RELEVANT DSP PLATFORM. SOME JURISDICTIONS DO NOT ALLOW LIMITATIONS ON IMPLIED WARRANTIES, SO THE ABOVE LIMITATIONS MAY NOT APPLY TO YOU.
terms.liabilityText: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, QUICKSEND SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR (A) ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOST PROFITS OR LOST STREAMING REVENUE ARISING FROM THE ACTIONS OR INACTION OF ANY DSP PLATFORM, OR (B) ANY AMOUNT EXCEEDING THE GREATER OF (I) $100 USD OR (II) THE AMOUNTS ACTUALLY PAID BY YOU TO QUICKSEND DURING THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. SOME JURISDICTIONS DO NOT ALLOW SUCH LIMITATIONS, SO THEY MAY NOT APPLY TO YOU.
terms.assignmentText: You may not assign your rights or obligations under these Terms to any third party without QuickSend's prior written consent. QuickSend may assign its rights and obligations under these Terms in connection with a reorganization, sale of business, or similar transaction without restriction.
terms.arbitrationText: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any dispute arising out of or relating to these Terms that cannot be resolved through negotiation shall be finally settled in Chicago, Illinois by a single arbitrator under the then-current JAMS Streamlined Arbitration Rules. Either party may seek injunctive or other equitable relief in court at any time. Arbitration shall be conducted on an individual basis only; class arbitrations and class actions are not permitted.
terms.miscellaneousText: You are responsible for paying all taxes and fees associated with your use of the Services. No failure by either party to exercise any right under these Terms constitutes a waiver of that right. If any provision of these Terms is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in full force. These Terms constitute the entire agreement between you and QuickSend regarding their subject matter and supersede all prior agreements on that subject.

privacy.title: QuickSend Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: QuickSend is a service for removing music tracks, cover art, and videos from DSP platforms based on copyright infringement complaints. Below we describe what data we collect when you submit a takedown request, how we use it, and who we share it with.
privacy.overviewText: To submit a copyright infringement complaint on your behalf, we need you to provide links to the content that needs to be removed, along with documents proving your rights to the work. We use this data strictly to prepare and submit the complaint to the relevant DSP platform and to track the status of your request.
privacy.dataCollectedText: When you register, we collect your email and basic Google profile information (if you sign in with Google). When you submit a takedown request, we collect: links to the track, cover art, or video to be removed; the platform where the content is hosted; and documents proving authorship or rights to the work (contracts, split sheets, copyright registration certificates, correspondence with a distributor, etc.). We do not collect or store payment details (card numbers, etc.) — these are processed directly by our payment providers (Stripe, PayPal, YooKassa).
privacy.howWeUseText: We use the data you provide to verify the validity of your request, prepare and submit an official copyright infringement complaint to the relevant DSP platform, show request status in your account dashboard, communicate with you about your request, and process payments. We do not use your data for advertising or marketing outreach without your separate consent.
privacy.documentStorageText1: Documents proving your rights to a work are stored on QuickSend's secured servers for as long as needed to process your request, and for some time afterward in case the DSP platform initiates a counter-notice process or requires further proof of rights.
privacy.documentStorageText2: We do not publish these documents or share them with third parties, except by submitting them directly to the relevant DSP platform as an attachment to the complaint, or where disclosure is required by law.
privacy.documentStorageText3: You may request deletion of documents we hold at any time, provided they are no longer required for an active or potentially disputed request.
privacy.thirdParty: Do We Share Data With Third Parties?
privacy.thirdPartyText: We share content links and supporting documents with the relevant DSP platforms (such as Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and others) solely as part of submitting a copyright infringement complaint. Payment processing is handled by Stripe, PayPal, and YooKassa in accordance with their own privacy policies. Sign-in via Google is handled in accordance with Google's privacy policy. We do not sell or share your data with third parties for advertising purposes.
privacy.dataDeletionText: You may request deletion of your personal data and uploaded documents from our servers at any time, except where retention is required to meet legal obligations or to resolve an active dispute related to an already-submitted complaint. To make such a request, please email
privacy.dataDeletionSuffix: from the QuickSend account whose data you want removed.
privacy.securityText: We use technical and organizational measures (encryption of data in transit, restricted internal access to documents) to protect the data you provide from unauthorized access.

basics.whichPlatforms: Which platforms do you remove content from?

process.howItWorks: How does the removal process work?
process.documentsNeeded: What documents do I need to provide?
process.trackStatus: How can I track my request?

payment.howMuch: How much does one takedown cost?
payment.bundles: Is there a discount for ordering multiple takedowns?
payment.paymentMethods: What payment methods do you accept?

legal.mustOwnRights: Do I need to be the rights holder to submit a request?
legal.falseClaim: What happens if I submit an inaccurate request?
legal.counterNotice: What if the uploader disputes the removal?
legal.noGuarantee: Do you guarantee 100% removal of content?

basics.whatIsQuickSend: QuickSend helps rights holders remove their music, cover art, and videos from DSP platforms when they've been uploaded without permission. We manually prepare and submit official copyright infringement complaints on your behalf.
basics.whatCanBeRemoved: Tracks, beats, covers, album/single artwork, and music videos that have been uploaded to DSP platforms without your consent as the rights holder.
basics.whichPlatforms: We submit complaints to major DSP platforms, including Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and other major music services. A single order covers removal across every platform where infringing content is found.

process.howItWorks: You submit a request through your account dashboard, providing links to the content and documents proving your rights. Our team manually reviews the materials, prepares an official copyright infringement complaint, and submits it to the relevant DSP platform. Your request status updates in your dashboard.
process.documentsNeeded: Typically we need documents proving your authorship or rights to the work: a contract, split sheet, copyright registration certificate, correspondence with a distributor, or other material that clearly links you to the work.
process.howLong: Timing depends on the specific DSP platform and its internal review process, so we can't guarantee an exact timeframe. We submit the complaint promptly once documents are verified — the further decision and timing are up to the platform.
process.trackStatus: Each request's status (in review, submitted, removed, rejected) is shown in your account dashboard at quicksend.vercel.app.

payment.howMuch: A single takedown (removal of a track across all associated DSP platforms) costs $25 USD.
payment.bundles: Yes. A bundle of 5 takedowns costs $110 USD, and a bundle of 10 takedowns costs $190 USD.
payment.paymentMethods: We accept payment via Stripe, PayPal, and YooKassa.
payment.refund: Yes — if a request hasn't been submitted to a DSP platform yet, the corresponding unused credit can be refunded. Credits for requests that have already been submitted are non-refundable, since the work has already been performed.

legal.mustOwnRights: Yes. By submitting a request, you confirm that you are the rights holder of the work or are authorized to act on their behalf, and you're required to provide supporting documentation.
legal.falseClaim: Knowingly submitting a false copyright infringement claim can result in legal liability to third parties and is grounds for refusing service or suspending your QuickSend account.
legal.counterNotice: If the uploader disputes the complaint through a counter-notice on the platform itself, the final decision rests with the DSP platform under its own policy. We may ask you for additional documents to strengthen your case.
legal.noGuarantee: No, we cannot guarantee a 100% outcome, since the decision to remove content rests with the relevant DSP platform, not QuickSend.

support.getSupport: Contact us at quicksendcontact@gmail.com or through your account dashboard.

dsp.title: DSP Takedowns
dsp.description: Someone uploaded your beat, cover art or video without permission? We handle the entire takedown process — from filing the complaint to confirming removal from the platform.
dsp.protectionDesc: We protect your rights across Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube, SoundCloud, Tidal, Deezer, Amazon Music and more.
dsp.originalityDesc: We help you document proof of original authorship to support your claim.
dsp.removalDesc: We file formal takedown requests and track removal status until the content is gone.
dsp.legalDesc: All takedowns are filed in compliance with DMCA and platform-specific policies.
dsp.guarantee: 100% legal, compliant with DMCA and platform rules

tenty.legalWarning: By submitting this form, you are making a legal declaration under penalty of perjury. Providing false or misleading information may result in legal consequences, including but not limited to civil penalties and criminal charges.
tenty.reportingReasonPlaceholder: Describe how your copyright was violated. Mention the track/content name, release date, and why you believe it infringes your rights. Minimum 50 characters.
tenty.evidenceUrlHelp: Provide a link to evidence proving your ownership (e.g., copyright registration, distribution agreement, etc.)
tenty.ownershipExplanationPlaceholder: Describe how you created or own this content. Include release date, original publication link, or any other proof. Minimum 100 characters.
tenty.agreeTermsTitle: I agree to the Terms of Service
tenty.agreeTermsText: I have read and agree to the platform's Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and Copyright Policy. I understand that this submission is subject to review and may take several weeks to process.
tenty.agreeAccuracyTitle: I confirm the accuracy of information
tenty.agreeAccuracyText: I certify that all information provided in this report is accurate, truthful, and complete to the best of my knowledge. I understand that providing false information may result in termination of my account and legal action.
tenty.agreePenaltiesText: I understand that making false claims may subject me to legal penalties including but not limited to: civil damages, legal fees, and in some jurisdictions, criminal charges for perjury or fraud.
tenty.submitSuccess: Report submitted successfully! You will receive a confirmation email shortly.
tenty.submitError: Error submitting report. Please try again.
tenty.invalidUrl: Must be a valid URL starting with http:// or https://
tenty.bio: Jordan Tennant is a Music Entrepreneur who is the Co Founder of Tenty. He helps producers and songwriters with DSP takedowns. 3 years experience in Music Industry
